Off Calendar Oct-09-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. JONATHAN C. TELLER, Esq. of Wilshire Law Firm (Tel: 213-381-9988) for Plaintiff Bryan Joseph Umili;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF BRYAN UMILI's Motion To Compel Defendant's Further Response To Form Interrogatories No. 13.2.

Plaintiff's counsel stipulates to the matter being decided by the Judge Pro Tem. The Judge Pro Tem and plaintiff's counsel authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

No appearance by or for defendant. Matter not contested/argued.

The Court adopts its tentative ruling, and rules as follows: Plaintiff's Motion to Compel is GRANTED. Information responsive to form interrogatory No. 13.2 is not automatically entitled to absolute or qualified work product protection. (See Coito v. Superior Court (2012) 54 Cal.4th 480, 502.) In addition, the Court finds that Defendant has not made the requisite foundational showing that any of the requested information would reveal work product. Although the Court agrees with Defendant that the investigative report(s) at issue may be entitled to absolute or qualified work product protection, the instant motion does not seek their production. Furthermore, to the extent that disclosure of the title of the report(s) discloses work product, Defendant may omit that information from his response. Defendant's request for monetary sanctions is DENIED.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the court's ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem at mpritt@bsfllp.com.
